senses.definition: the weight of a person’s own body, without any added equipment or load
examples: Push-ups use your bodyweight for resistance.
The trainer measured his bodyweight to track progress.

senses.definition: concerning or in relation to the body; from a bodily or physical perspective
examples: She considers health from a bodywise viewpoint.
Bodywise, his training was very rigorous.

senses.definition: a term that may refer to a type of wood from a tree in the genus Calophyllum or a fictional/rare usage; not widely established in mainstream lexicon
examples: The furniture was made from bodywood, a rare timber.

senses.definition: the work done on a car’s body or on the human body; also therapy focusing on the body
examples: The car needs some bodywork after the accident.
She received bodywork to relieve tension in her shoulders.

senses.definition: a person who practices bodywork therapies or manual therapies on the body
examples: The clinic employs several professional bodyworkers.

senses.definition: plural of bodyworker; multiple practitioners of bodywork therapies
examples: The center hires several bodyworkers from different disciplines.

senses.definition: the field or practice of bodywork; or the body of work related to body construction or care
examples: He studied bodyworks to understand therapeutic massage.

senses.definition: an abbreviation or acronym in various contexts; may refer to Bank of England entries, Board of Education, or be a nonstandard abbreviation in some languages; meaning depends on context
examples: The boe value was updated in the report.

senses.definition: a term with limited or obscure usage; may refer to a proper noun from a specific culture or a misspelling; not widely recognized in general English
examples: Boebera appears in some regional texts.

senses.definition: a historical or scholarly term likely referencing ancient Greek heraldry or a specific festival; not common in modern everyday English
examples: The inscription mentioned boedromion events.

senses.definition: a reference to Boehmenism, a historical or obscure religious or philosophical movement associated with Boehme; not common in contemporary usage
examples: Scholars discussed Boehmenism in the treatise.

senses.definition: a follower of Boehmenism; a rare or historical term
examples: The Boehmenist wrote about metaphysical topics.

senses.definition: a mineral form of aluminum oxide hydroxide used in industry and geology
examples: Boehmite is often used as a precursor in aluminum production.

senses.definition: plural of boehmite; multiple minerals of Boehmite
examples: Samples contained several boehmites.

senses.definition: Boeing, an American multinational company that designs and manufactures airplanes and related products
examples: Boeing unveiled a new airliner at the show.

senses.definition: an ancient Athenian military or political rank, leader of the Boeotians in some contexts; historically specific
examples: The boeotarch gave a strategic command.

senses.definition: a region in central Greece; historical term often used in classical studies
examples: Boecia is known for its ancient ruins; Boeotia played a role in Greek history.

senses.definition: relating to Boeotia or its people; also used in classics to describe the dialect or culture
examples: Boeotian dialect is described in ancient texts.

senses.definition: of or relating to Boeotia; variant form of Boeotian
examples: Boeotic inscriptions illuminate ancient Boeotia.

senses.definition: an informal or obscure term; may refer to an acronym or name in specific contexts
examples: The term boep appeared in a niche glossary.

senses.definition: plural form of boep; used in niche contexts
examples: Several boeps were listed in the glossary.

senses.definition: a member of a South African farming community of Dutch descent; can also refer to an Afrikaans-speaking farmer
examples: The boer community played a key role in farming history.

senses.definition: informal or historically loaded term that can be considered pejorative; use with care
examples: The term boer may be sensitive in some contexts.

senses.definition: an obscure or compound term; not widely used in modern English
examples: Boerbul is not common in contemporary texts.
